reasoning:
The baseline price is the market close on Friday, March 28, 2025. Crucially, Altria (MO) went ex-dividend ($1.02/share) on March 25, meaning this baseline price is mechanically depressed relative to its long-term total return. Over the period between now and the next ex-dividend date (expected mid-June 2025), the unadjusted stock price will accrete value (accumulated earnings) without any cash distributions. This creates a mild structural positive drift for the first two resolution dates (April 6 and April 29), offsetting the drag of being a high-yield stock. 

By June 28, the June ex-dividend will have occurred, dropping the unadjusted price back down. Similarly, by the September 2025 and March 2026 dates, subsequent quarterly dividends will keep the unadjusted capital appreciation roughly flat, aligning with Altria's historical pattern of generating returns almost exclusively through dividends rather than price action. Given the high noise inherent in single-stock paths and the long-term convergence of unadjusted price expectations to near-zero drift, probabilities for dates progressively further out seamlessly converge to 50%.
